creator: Entrepreneurship | Startups | Innovation
caption:
The perfect salesman doesn’t brag, pitch, or overwhelm.
He says almost nothing — he just asks the right questions.

Why?
Because people trust their own conclusions far more than your explanations.
When a prospect talks, they reveal their needs, pain points, fears, and desires.
When you talk too much, you’re guessing.
When they talk, they’re selling themselves.

Smart salespeople guide with questions like:
	•	“What’s the biggest challenge you’re trying to fix?”
	•	“What would the ideal solution look like for you?”
	•	“Why is solving this important right now?”

Each question sharpens the prospect’s clarity — and brings them closer to a “yes.”
Because the golden rule of persuasion is simple:
People don’t believe what you tell them.
They believe what they tell themselves.
